title: Group Relative Preference Optimization
description: A groupwise alignment training regime that samples multiple candidate answers for the same prompt and updates the model from how those answers rank relative to one another.
openingSummary: Group Relative Preference Optimization, usually shortened to GRPO, is a post-training alignment method that samples several candidate answers for the same prompt, scores them as a group, and nudges the model toward answers that rank higher relative to the other samples in that group.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Group Relative Preference Optimization is a reinforcement-style alignment regime for language models. Instead of learning from one fixed preferred-versus-rejected pair per prompt, it draws a small group of candidate answers from the current policy, compares them against one another, and uses that within-group ranking as the training signal.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: Many alignment pipelines need policy updates that react to freshly sampled model behavior, not only to comparisons frozen in a dataset. GRPO exists so teams can shape post-training from grouped relative rankings while avoiding some of the extra machinery that classic reinforcement learning from human feedback loops often carry, such as maintaining a separate value model alongside the policy.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: For each training prompt, the current model samples multiple candidate answers. A reward or preference signal scores those answers, and GRPO turns the scores into relative advantages inside the group: answers that beat the group average are reinforced, while weaker answers are discouraged. The policy update follows that grouped comparison rather than a single static pair.

comparedToNearbyRegimes.title: Compared To Nearby Regimes
comparedToNearbyRegimes.body: Compared with the broad reinforcement learning from human feedback workflow, GRPO is a specific grouped optimization pattern used inside post-training rather than the whole alignment story. Compared with Direct Preference Optimization, GRPO learns from sampled answer groups for the same prompt instead of relying only on stored chosen-versus-rejected pairs.

limitationsAndFailureModes.title: Limitations And Failure Modes
limitationsAndFailureModes.body: GRPO depends on the quality of the reward or preference signal applied to each sampled group. If scoring is noisy, too sparse, or gameable, the grouped update can reinforce shallow patterns. Sampling several answers per prompt also adds rollout cost relative to methods that train directly from fixed preference datasets.

trainingFlow.title: Group Relative Preference Optimization training flow
trainingFlow.alt: A three-step GRPO flow: prompt with multiple sampled answers, grouped relative objective, then the updated aligned model.
trainingFlow.caption: GRPO compares sampled answers within each prompt group before applying the policy update.
